pattern: Scan for each strictly increasing run that is immediately followed by a strictly decreasing run, measuring the combined length, to find the longest peak-shaped subarray in one pass.
hints: A mountain needs a strict climb up to a peak and then a strict descent; flat steps break it.
Find positions that are true peaks, where the neighbor on each side is strictly lower.

From each peak expand outward while values keep strictly increasing on the left and strictly decreasing on the right, then take the longest total width; alternatively track up/down run lengths in a single sweep.

edge_inputs.description: one clear mountain inside a longer array
all equal values form no mountain
minimal valid mountain of length three
a plateau at the peak disqualifies it
two mountains, the later one longer

stress.description: small value range so plateaus and short runs appear often
